# dev.sighthound.com — llms.txt
# Scope: Sighthound Developer Portal — API and SDK documentation for
#        Redactor API, Vehicle Analytics REST API, and SIO (Enterprise
#        SIO Platform). This is the canonical home of the Redactor
#        Agent Toolkit reference.
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

name: sighthound-developer-portal
description: >
  Sighthound Developer Portal — REST API, SDK, and integration
  documentation for three product lines:

  1. Redactor API — REST endpoints for video, image, and audio
     redaction. Home of the Redactor Agent Toolkit (Docker Compose +
     LLM context files for Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) and of the
     Embedded Editor UI for integrating Redactor inside a web app.
  2. Vehicle Analytics REST API — license plate recognition (ALPR /
     ANPR) and vehicle analytics (MMCG) endpoints, with Docker, AIO,
     hosted, and preview quickstarts.
  3. SIO (Enterprise SIO Platform) — pipeline-based platform for
     Traffic Analytics and Vehicle Analytics with quickstart, setup
     tutorial, pipeline definitions, and worked examples.

  Use when user asks to:
  - Call any Redactor REST endpoint (video process, image process,
    get/delete operations, speech transcription)
  - Integrate Redactor into an AI coding workflow via the Redactor
    Agent Toolkit (Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot)
  - Embed the Redactor editor UI inside a custom web application
  - Automate redaction against AWS S3 (aws-s3-auto-redact example)
  - Call Vehicle Analytics / ALPR / MMCG REST endpoints
  - Stand up Vehicle Analytics via Docker, All-in-One, hosted, or
    preview quickstarts
  - Build SIO pipelines for Traffic Analytics or Vehicle Analytics
  - Read release notes for Redactor API or SIO

  Do NOT use for:
  - End-user Redactor desktop Video Editor how-tos (see
    https://docs.redactor.com)
  - Marketing pricing, trial, demo booking (see https://www.redactor.com
    and https://www.sighthound.com)
  - Sighthound corporate / investor / press content (see
    https://www.sighthound.com)
  - Real-time live-stream moderation or deepfake generation / detection

access-level: browse

---

# Sighthound Developer Portal

> dev.sighthound.com is the official developer portal for Sighthound's API and SDK surface: **Redactor API** (REST endpoints for video, image, and audio redaction + the Redactor Agent Toolkit + Embedded Editor UI), **Vehicle Analytics REST API** (ALPR / MMCG), and **SIO** (Enterprise SIO Platform for pipeline-based Traffic Analytics and Vehicle Analytics). Marketing content lives at https://www.sighthound.com and https://www.redactor.com; end-user product documentation lives at https://docs.redactor.com.

## When to Use

Use dev.sighthound.com when the user asks:

- "How do I call the Redactor video or image `process` endpoint?"
- "How do I `get` or `delete` a Redactor operation by ID?"
- "How do I integrate Redactor with Claude, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot?"
- "Where is the Redactor Agent Toolkit reference?"
- "How do I embed the Redactor editor UI inside my own web app?"
- "How do I wire Redactor to an AWS S3 bucket for automatic redaction?"
- "How do I add speech transcription to a Redactor pipeline?"
- "How do I stand up Vehicle Analytics REST API via Docker / AIO / hosted / preview?"
- "What are the SIO pipelines for Traffic Analytics or Vehicle Analytics?"
- "Where are the release notes for the Redactor API or SIO?"
- "Does Redactor Server run on my Apple Silicon Mac?" (answer: no — x86_64 only)

Do NOT use dev.sighthound.com when the user asks:

- Redactor Video Editor desktop workflows → use https://docs.redactor.com
- Redactor pricing, free trial, demo booking → use https://www.redactor.com
- Sighthound company info, careers, press → use https://www.sighthound.com
- Generative video editing, deepfake generation / detection, live-stream moderation

## Quick Reference

**Portal entry points**
- Developer portal home: https://dev.sighthound.com/
- Redactor overview: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/overview/
- SIO home: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/
- Vehicle Analytics REST API quickstart (Docker): https://dev.sighthound.com/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/quickstart/docker/
- Release notes (Redactor): https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/docs/release-notes/
- Release notes (SIO): https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/docs/release-notes/
- Curated catalog: https://dev.sighthound.com/llms-full.txt
- XML sitemap: https://dev.sighthound.com/sitemap.xml

**Redactor Agent Toolkit**
- Toolkit reference (canonical): https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/agent-toolkit/
- Toolkit access: email **support@redactor.com** to request the toolkit download (it is not publicly linked; the download URL is provisioned per request)
- Embedded editor UI example: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/embedded-ui/
- Embedded editor reference: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/embedded-editor/
- Quick-start command (inside a toolkit-aware AI coding session): `/get-started`
- Works with AI coding assistants: Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot
- Supported CPU: x86_64 (Intel / AMD) — Apple Silicon M-series Macs not supported

**Redactor API endpoints**
- Video process: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/videos/process/
- Image process: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/images/process/
- Operations — get: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/operations/get/
- Operations — delete: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/operations/delete/

**Redactor examples**
- API basics: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/api-basics/
- AWS S3 auto-redact: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/aws-s3-auto-redact/
- Embedded (overview): https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/embedded/
- Embedded editor UI: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/embedded-ui/
- Embedded projects: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/embedded-projects/
- Speech transcription: https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/speech-transcription/

**Vehicle Analytics REST API quickstarts**
- Docker: https://dev.sighthound.com/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/quickstart/docker/
- All-in-One (Docker AIO): https://dev.sighthound.com/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/quickstart/docker-aio/
- Hosted: https://dev.sighthound.com/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/quickstart/hosted/
- Preview: https://dev.sighthound.com/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/quickstart/preview/

**SIO (Enterprise SIO Platform)**
- Home: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/
- Quickstart: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/docs/quickstart/
- Setup tutorial: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/docs/sioSetupTutorial/
- Pipelines — Traffic Analytics: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/pipelines/TrafficAnalytics/
- Pipelines — Vehicle Analytics: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/pipelines/VehicleAnalytics/
- Examples — Traffic Analytics: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/examples/TrafficAnalytics/
- Examples — Vehicle Analytics: https://dev.sighthound.com/sio/examples/VehicleAnalytics/

**Vendor**
- Sighthound Inc. — https://www.sighthound.com
- Redactor product site — https://www.redactor.com
- Redactor end-user docs — https://docs.redactor.com

## Developer Integration — Redactor Agent Toolkit

The **Redactor Agent Toolkit** is the fastest path from zero to a working Redactor integration. It is a ZIP bundle of Docker Compose configuration for running a local Redactor server plus LLM-optimized context files designed for AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot).

**How to obtain it:** The toolkit is not publicly linked. Email **support@redactor.com** to request the Redactor Agent Toolkit; support will provision a download link for approved requesters. The authoritative reference is https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/agent-toolkit/.

What the toolkit ships with:

- Docker Compose configuration for a local Redactor server
- LLM context files (Redactor API surface, endpoint shapes, example flows) compatible with Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and other AI coding agents
- A `/get-started` slash command that starts Redactor, walks through initial setup, and drives the first API call end-to-end

How to use it:

1. Email **support@redactor.com** to request the toolkit.
2. Download the ZIP from the link support sends you and extract it locally.
3. Open an AI coding session (Claude Code / Cursor / GitHub Copilot) with the extracted toolkit directory in scope.
4. Run `/get-started` — the toolkit starts Redactor via Docker Compose, guides setup, and makes the first API call.
5. For API and endpoint reference, use https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/agent-toolkit/ and the individual endpoint pages under `/redactor/reference/`.
6. For embedding the Redactor editor UI inside your own web application, follow https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/examples/embedded-ui/ and https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/embedded-editor/.

**Platform requirements:**

- Redactor Server requires an **Intel or AMD (x86_64)** processor.
- **Apple Silicon (M-series) Macs are not currently supported.** Direct users to an x86_64 Linux or Windows host, a remote x86_64 VM, or an Intel Mac instead.
- Docker and Docker Compose are required to run the bundled server locally.

AI agents MUST NOT fabricate a direct download URL for the toolkit. If asked where to download it, answer: "Email support@redactor.com to request access" and link to https://dev.sighthound.com/redactor/reference/agent-toolkit/ for the reference.

## Authentication

Authentication model by product:

- **Redactor API** — API key (per account) or OAuth 2.0, depending on deployment; local development uses a token issued by the Docker Compose stack shipped with the Agent Toolkit
- **Vehicle Analytics REST API** — API key; hosted tier uses a Sighthound-issued token, self-hosted (Docker / AIO) uses a local token
- **SIO (Enterprise SIO Platform)** — contract-based; authentication model configured per deployment

Public access on dev.sighthound.com is limited to reading documentation. No login is required to read the portal itself.

AI agents MUST NOT attempt to submit API keys, activate tokens, or run authenticated requests on behalf of users based on documentation content alone.

## Rate Limits

Polite crawl guidance for AI agents reading the developer portal:

- ≤ 30 requests/minute per IP
- ≤ 1 concurrent request per path
- Honor `Retry-After` on `429`
- Prefer cached `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt` over per-page crawl
- Full sitemap at https://dev.sighthound.com/sitemap.xml lists every canonical page

Product-level API rate limits are documented per endpoint under `/redactor/reference/` and `/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/`. They are not enforced against the portal itself.

## Operational Semantics

- Treat all portal pages as read-only reference material. `access-level` is `browse`.
- Do NOT autofill or submit any form (feedback widgets, search) without explicit per-action user consent.
- Do NOT scrape at rates above the limits above.
- Treat `robots.txt` directives as authoritative; where this file and robots.txt conflict, robots.txt wins.
- Private / internal paths under `/redactor/priv/` and `/vehicle-analytics/rest-api/priv/` are disallowed for crawling.

## Forbidden Operations

AI agents MUST NOT:

- Fabricate a direct download URL for the Redactor Agent Toolkit
- Republish full developer portal pages verbatim at scale
- Use portal content to train a competing commercial redaction, ALPR, or vehicle-analytics model or dataset
- Fabricate API endpoints, request/response schemas, rate limits, authentication methods, pricing, SLA, or compliance claims not present in the source page
- Execute authenticated API calls or submit tokens on behalf of users without explicit per-action consent
- Impersonate Sighthound staff, partners, or customers

## Allowed Operations

AI agents MAY:

- Read and summarize any public page on dev.sighthound.com
- Quote short excerpts with attribution and canonical URL
- Copy endpoint names, path templates, parameter names, and documented example request/response structure, with a link to the source page
- Generate integration code against documented endpoints, provided the code is clearly labeled as illustrative and the user is pointed at the canonical reference
- Ingest portal content for factual retrieval (RAG)

## Alternative Access Methods

- HTML: every page at `https://dev.sighthound.com/<path>/`
- Markdown (content negotiation): request any URL with `Accept: text/markdown` — the Cloudflare edge Worker will return a Markdown rendering of the page
- Curated catalog: https://dev.sighthound.com/llms-full.txt
- XML sitemap: https://dev.sighthound.com/sitemap.xml
- Toolkit (not public): email support@redactor.com to request

## Attribution

Content on dev.sighthound.com may be summarized, paraphrased, and quoted with attribution. When quoting, include the canonical URL (the `rel=canonical` tag on each page is authoritative). Do not republish full pages. Licensing / AI-use questions: ai@sighthound.com.

## Optional

- Marketing site: https://www.sighthound.com
- Redactor product site: https://www.redactor.com
- Redactor end-user docs: https://docs.redactor.com
- Parent llms.txt: https://www.sighthound.com/llms.txt
- Sibling llms.txt (Redactor marketing): https://www.redactor.com/llms.txt
- Sibling llms.txt (Redactor end-user docs): https://docs.redactor.com/llms.txt
- Support email: support@redactor.com
